title: centaurea drabifolia subsp. austro-occidentalis
intro: Centaurea drabifolia subsp. austro-occidentalis, also known as the Western button centaury, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its small, button-like flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Centaurea drabifolia subsp. austro-occidentalis include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
